title: Agelanthus polygonifolius
taxonomy: Agelanthus polygonifolius is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Loranthaceae. It belongs to the genus Agelanthus, which is a group of parasitic plants. Specifically, A. polygonifolius is a species of mistletoe, which is a type of hemiparasitic plant that derives some of its nutrients from a host plant. This classification places it within the order Santalales, a group of plants known for their parasitic or semi-parasitic habits. Further, A. polygonifolius is native to tropical Africa, where it can be found growing on various host plants.
